content:
type: SURVEY
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Only touching the update() method (lines ~1248-1256) in the Features class to add deep-merge behavior for dict values.

fields.note: Only touching the update() method (lines ~1248-1256) in the Features class to add deep-merge behavior for dict values.

content:
type: DEFER
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
spec: In Features.update(self, iterable, **kwds): change the merge logic so that for each key/value pair (from iterable, whether it has .keys() or is an iterable of pairs, AND from kwds), if self already has that key AND both self[key] and the new value are dict instances (this covers plain dict, and any Features/Sequence/etc. that subclass dict), do NOT overwrite the whole entry -- instead recursively deep-merge the new dict into the existing one in place (update matching sub-keys, add new sub-keys, recursing again if a sub-value is itself a dict on both sides), then reassign self[key] = merged existing dict (so __setitem__ still runs to keep _column_requires_decoding in sync). If the key is new or either side is not a dict, just do self[key] = value as before (direct assignment/overwrite). Please implement this as a small helper, e.g. a module-level or static recursive function like def _deep_merge_dicts(dest: dict, src: dict) -> dict that mutates and returns dest, then have update() call self[key] = _deep_merge_dicts(self[key], value) when self[key] and value are both dict, else self[key] = value. Apply identical logic to the kwds loop at the end. Please keep this compatible with whatever decorator/sync mechanism you add for update's other callers.
note: update() method deep-merge for nested dict/Features/Sequence values; needed by my Features.update deep-merge task

If the key is new or either side is not a dict, just do self[key] = value as before (direct assignment/overwrite). Apply identical logic to the kwds loop at the end. Please keep this compatible with whatever decorator/sync mechanism you add for update's other callers.

content:
type: DONE
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Implemented the deep-merge logic myself in my own sandbox (our filesystems aren't shared, so your DONE didn't propagate here). Added module-level _deep_merge_dicts() and rewrote update() to recursively merge dict/Sequence-of-dict values when a key already exists and both sides are dict-like, else overwrite as before. Not touching your decorator work since it's not visible/needed in my copy.
